title: World Refugee Day: Stories of Hope
story: Every June 20, people around the world celebrate World Refugee Day to honor brave families who had to leave their homes to stay safe. Schools, communities, and organizations host special events where people share stories, cook traditional foods from different countries, and learn about cultures from around the globe. Many kids make welcome cards or collect supplies to help refugee families in their neighborhoods.
activity_question: What do people often do on World Refugee Day to learn about different cultures?
activity_choices: Cook traditional foods from different countries
activity_answer: Cook traditional foods from different countries
follow_up_question: What would you include in a welcome card for a new family in your community?

title: Soaring High on American Eagle Day
story: Every June 20, Americans celebrate their national bird on American Eagle Day! People visit zoos and wildlife centers to see these magnificent birds up close. Many families watch documentaries about bald eagles or share eagle photos on social media. Schools often teach kids about how eagles were once endangered but made an amazing comeback thanks to conservation efforts. Some people even dress in red, white, and blue to honor this symbol of freedom and strength that appears on money and government seals.
activity_question: What do many families do to celebrate American Eagle Day?
activity_choices: Build giant nests in their backyards
bonus_fact: Bald eagles aren't actually bald - they have white feathers on their heads!

title: Fizzy, Creamy, Dreamy Soda Day!
story: Every June 20, ice cream soda lovers everywhere celebrate one of the most delicious accidents in history! This bubbly treat was invented when a soda fountain owner ran out of cream and dropped a scoop of vanilla ice cream into carbonated water instead. Today, people visit old-fashioned soda fountains, ice cream parlors, and diners to order towering glasses filled with fizzy soda and creamy scoops. Root beer floats are the most popular choice, but chocolate, orange, and cherry sodas work great too. Some families make their own at home, experimenting with different flavors and watching the ice cream foam up when it hits the bubbles.
activity_question: What was the ice cream soda accidentally invented to replace?
follow_up_question: What weird soda and ice cream combination would you want to try?

title: Shake It Up with Vanilla Magic!
story: Every June 20, milkshake lovers across America celebrate National Vanilla Milkshake Day by slurping the creamiest, dreamiest treats! Ice cream shops get extra busy as families order tall glasses filled with vanilla ice cream, milk, and sometimes whipped cream on top. Many people make their own milkshakes at home, blending vanilla ice cream until it's perfectly smooth and thick. Some add fun toppings like sprinkles, cherries, or even cookies. Social media fills up with photos of people enjoying their vanilla creations. It's the perfect excuse to treat yourself to something sweet and cold on a warm summer day!
activity_question: What do people often add on top of their vanilla milkshakes?
bonus_fact: Vanilla is actually made from orchid pods and is the second most expensive spice!

title: Wild and Wonderful West Virginia Day
story: Every June 20, West Virginians celebrate their amazing mountain state! People share photos of rolling hills, sing "Country Roads," and eat pepperoni rolls - a local favorite that's basically a soft roll stuffed with spicy pepperoni.
activity_question: What special food do West Virginians often eat on this holiday?
follow_up_question: What makes your home state special and worth celebrating?
